content.claim: Public per-pupil financing is identical across elite selective gimnáziums and disadvantaged rural or segregated schools, yet entrance to elite tracks depends substantially on private tutoring and test preparation rather than raw ability alone.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Per-pupil financing equality stated as institutional fact but specific budget amounts not provided in notes
Private tutoring prevalence and its causal role in track access asserted but not quantified
No formal fiscal-incidence study comparing outcomes per public forint across school types found in available sources
Framing as equity problem is value-laden inference by interviewee rather than settled economic measure
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
